title: Expose operator readback and visible resize controls in the plugin UI
summary: 0.6.14 improves the player plugin panel as a live operator test surface by surfacing current version, target, media, timeline, and play-state readback, and by adding visible resize controls that already existed in the runtime contract.
reasoning: With 0.6.13 correcting the preset overlap, the next safest quality rung is the plugin UI itself. The operator needs a more truthful test surface while validating the current raw dev_deploy alpha in VR, but this slice should not destabilize playback transport, Meta interaction, or scene generation. Exposing existing runtime readback and already-implemented resize actions improves observability and operator control without changing the player core behavior.

changes.summary: The plugin UI now shows live build version, player target, media, timeline, and state readback fields.
changes.reasoning: These storables already existed and updated at runtime, but they were hidden from the operator panel.

changes.summary: The plugin UI now exposes Player Resize Down and Player Resize Up actions.
changes.reasoning: Resize already existed in the runtime and deterministic scene wiring; surfacing it in the plugin panel makes the test surface more complete.

changes.summary: The plugin UI now uses light spacer grouping so presets, readback, editable path/transport, and advanced sections are easier to read.
changes.reasoning: The current panel remains VaM-native, but small structure improvements make it more usable as a live test surface.

verification: Build the player runtime cleanly from vs/fa_cua_player/fa_cua_player.csproj.
Deploy the raw modern_tv 0.6.14 alpha proof and the baseline direct-player raw asset.
Confirm the plugin panel shows readback fields and visible resize controls while keeping the current raw dev_deploy seam authoritative.
Refresh the Volodeck witness packet so visual proof remains current while the operator tests the live raw alpha in VR.
knownIssues: This slice does not redesign the full plugin layout into a custom grid system; it remains a VaM-native row flow.
This slice does not promote the Meta runtime surface to full interaction; that remains a later lane.
This slice does not change packaged prerelease authority; the live test seam remains raw dev_deploy.
